evidence_excerpt: The girl died at the age of 20 months, affected by pneumonia and acute respiratory distress syndrome.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Despite reduction of ‘big’ seizures, she continued to have 6–30 eye/head deviations per day and later 3–4 focal seizures daily, with transient worsening during intercurrent infections.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: After sodium bromide (up to 80 mg/kg), within 2–3 weeks she had three to six seizures a day with arm jerks and fewer discreet seizures.
At 4 months, stiripentol was added; after 15 days, seizures with jerks were reduced to one per day.
She had transient worse episodes during intercurrent infections.
At 6.5 months, levetiracetam was introduced; with this combination she had no seizures with arm jerk or tonic elevation, but 6–30 eye/head deviations per day persisted.
She was discharged hypotonic and without psychomotor progress at the age of seven months.
Between 12 and 15 months, progressive microcephaly was noted.
During seizure worsening periods, she received i.v. midazolam, lorazepam, phenobarbitone, and add-on Chloraldurat.
Mechanical ventilation was used when needed, and she was fed by nasogastric tube.
Died at 20 months due to pneumonia and acute respiratory distress syndrome.

notes_on_uncertainty: The source describes two patients with MMPSI. This summary pertains to Patient 1 because her long-term outcome (death) is clearly documented. The diagnosis (index time) was considered after recognizing migrating ictal discharges approximately one month after admission (~3 months of age). Timing categories are approximated relative to this diagnosis point.
